name: va_add_range_zero_is_identity
code:
proof fn phi_1_va_add_range_zero_is_identity(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_va_add_range(va, 0) == va,
{
}
reason: spec_va_add_range(va, 0) = (va + 0*4096) = va by definition — but va_add_range's ensures only guarantees `ret != va` when `i != 0`, leaving the i==0 case consistent; this tests whether the spec correctly handles the identity case

name: va_4k_range_valid_vacuous_for_zero_len
reason: spec_va_4k_range_valid with len==0 is vacuously true for ANY va (even invalid ones) since the forall quantifier has an empty range — no va_4k_valid precondition is needed

name: va_add_range_no_overflow_check
code:
proof fn phi_3_va_add_range_no_overflow_check(va: usize, i: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va),
        i > 0,
    ensures
        spec_va_add_range(va, i) != va,
{
}
reason: va_add_range's external_body ensures `i != 0 ==> ret != va` without overflow checks — but spec_va_add_range computes `(va + i*4096) as usize` which can wrap around to equal va on overflow, creating a mismatch between spec and trusted ensures

name: va_4k_range_valid_does_not_imply_contiguous
code:
proof fn phi_4_va_4k_range_valid_does_not_imply_contiguous(va: usize, i: usize, j: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va),
        spec_va_4k_range_valid(va, 10),
        0 <= i < 10,
        0 <= j < 10,
        i != j,
    ensures
        spec_va_add_range(va, i) != spec_va_add_range(va, j),
{
}
reason: spec_va_add_range could wrap around modulo usize::MAX+1, making two distinct indices map to the same virtual address — the range validity check doesn't prevent collisions from arithmetic overflow

name: va_add_range_nonzero_ensures_without_bounds
code:
proof fn phi_5_va_add_range_nonzero_ensures_without_bounds(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_va_add_range(va, 1) != 0,
{
}
reason: va_add_range guarantees ret != va when i != 0, but nothing prevents the result from being 0 (null address) — adding 4096 to a valid VA could overflow to 0, yet the external_body ensures would still claim the result is non-equal to va
